description: Fix documentation inaccuracies found during a static audit comparing docs content against the actual source code. Covers missing/incorrect API signatures, stale code examples, wrong references, outdated illustration text, and incorrect example values.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add missing displayName to plugin-class.mdx class signature
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the OpenTabsPlugin class signature code block to show all abstract properties so that I know what I must implement.
acceptanceCriteria: The class signature code block in sdk/plugin-class.mdx includes `abstract readonly displayName: string` alongside the other abstract properties (name, version, description, urlPatterns, tools)
The ordering in the code block matches the source: name, displayName, version, description, urlPatterns, tools
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter is set to `2026-03-01`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes: File: docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx. The class signature code block (around lines 18-37) shows the abstract class definition but omits `abstract readonly displayName: string`. The source of truth is platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts line 222 where displayName is declared as `abstract readonly displayName: string`. The properties table later on the same page already lists displayName as required — only the code block is missing it. Add it after the `name` declaration to match the source order.

userStories.title: Remove incorrect `override` keyword from abstract member examples
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want code examples to use correct TypeScript syntax so that I don't mistakenly think `override` is required on abstract property implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: No occurrence of `override readonly displayName` in docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx — should be `readonly displayName`
No occurrence of `override readonly displayName` in docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx — should be `readonly displayName`
No occurrence of `override readonly displayName` in docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx — should be `readonly displayName`
No occurrence of `override async isReady()` in docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx — should be `async isReady()`
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter is set to `2026-03-01` on all three files
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Three files to edit:
1. docs/content/docs/first-plugin.mdx (around line 64) — change `override readonly displayName = '...'` to `readonly displayName = '...'`
2. docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx (around line 85) — same change for displayName
3. docs/content/docs/guides/resources-prompts.mdx (around line 500) — change `override readonly displayName` to `readonly displayName` AND change `override async isReady()` to `async isReady()`

The reason: displayName and isReady() are declared as `abstract` in the SDK source (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts lines 222, 228). Abstract members do NOT require the `override` keyword when implemented in a subclass — only non-abstract inherited members do. The resources-prompts.mdx page itself states this rule at line ~163 but violates it in its own full example.

userStories.title: Add missing `port` config field and `server.pid` to ConfigDirectory illustration
userStories.description: As a user, I want the configuration reference to document all config fields and the config directory illustration to show all files so that I have a complete picture of the config structure.
acceptanceCriteria: The Config fields table in configuration.mdx includes a `port` row with type `number`, default `9515`, and a description explaining it is the MCP server port persisted by `opentabs config set port`
The ConfigDirectory illustration component in illustrations.tsx includes a `server.pid` entry with description 'Server process ID'
The `server.pid` entry appears between `server.log` and the `extension/` directory section in the illustration
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter in configuration.mdx is set to `2026-03-01`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Two files to edit:
1. docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx — add a row to the Config fields table (around lines 16-58). The port field exists because `opentabs config set port <number>` writes it to config.json (see platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts lines 455-474). The CLI reads it back at platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts line 83. Note: this is a CLI-level setting used by commands like status/audit/logs to know which port to connect to. The MCP server reads its port from --port flag or environment, not from config.json.
2. docs/components/illustrations.tsx — find the ConfigDirectory component and add a `server.pid` file entry. The file is created by `opentabs start` (platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts line 363) and defined in platform/shared/src/constants.ts (getPidFilePath). Follow the existing pattern for file entries in the illustration (text elements with labels and descriptions). The server.pid should appear after server.log in the tree.

userStories.title: Fix troubleshooting.mdx auth.json reference and localhost in curl
userStories.description: As a user troubleshooting authentication issues, I want accurate guidance about where the secret is stored so that I don't delete the wrong file.
acceptanceCriteria: The authentication troubleshooting entry references `auth.json` (not `config.json`) as the file where the secret is stored
The parenthetical example says 'after deleting auth.json or rotating the secret' instead of 'after deleting config.json'
All curl examples in troubleshooting.mdx use `127.0.0.1` instead of `localhost`
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter is set to `2026-03-01`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx
Issue 1 (around line 31): The text says 'If the secret changed (e.g., after deleting config.json)' but the secret lives in ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json, not config.json. Deleting config.json would NOT affect the secret. Change to reference auth.json and mention `opentabs config rotate-secret --confirm` as the other way the secret changes.
Issue 2 (around line 102): The diagnostic commands table has `curl http://localhost:9515/health` — change to `curl http://127.0.0.1:9515/health`. The server binds to 127.0.0.1 (DEFAULT_HOST in platform/shared/src/constants.ts), and localhost may resolve to ::1 on some systems.

userStories.title: Fix mcp-server.mdx browserToolCount and localhost in curl examples
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the MCP server reference to show accurate example values and consistent URLs so that I can trust the documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: The example /health response shows `browserToolCount` as 40 (not 39)
If `toolCount` in the example is computed as a sum that includes browserToolCount, it is also updated accordingly
All curl examples in mcp-server.mdx use `127.0.0.1` instead of `localhost` (check /ws-info, /audit, /reload sections)
The wsUrl in the /ws-info example response uses `127.0.0.1` instead of `localhost`
The `lastUpdated` frontmatter is set to `2026-03-01`
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx
Issue 1 (around line 164): The example authenticated /health response shows `"browserToolCount": 39`. The actual count is 40 — see platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tool-names.ts which exports a BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES array with 40 entries. Update to 40. Check if `toolCount` in the same example includes browserToolCount and needs adjustment.
Issue 2: Multiple curl examples use `localhost` — search for all occurrences of `localhost` in the file and change to `127.0.0.1`. Key locations: /ws-info curl example (around line 215), /ws-info response wsUrl field, /audit curl example (around line 236), /reload curl example (around line 297). The server binds to DEFAULT_HOST which is '127.0.0.1' (platform/shared/src/constants.ts).
